name: Hungarian Sausage
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Pork butt, boneless - cut into large pieces
1 lb Beef chuck, cut into large pieces
1 lb Pork fat, fresh - cut into large pieces
10 Garlic cloves, peeled and crushed (about 2 tbsp)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a meat grinder, coarsely grind the pork, beef, and pork fat, in batches.
2. Add all remaining ingredients to the ground meat mixture, except the casings. Mix well and allow to sit while you clean the casings.
3. Rinse the casings thoroughly in cold water and run fresh water through them. Drain.
4. Using a sausage machine, a KitchenAid with a sausage attachment, or a sausage funnel, fill the casings and tie them off into about 16-inch lengths. Do not fill them too tightly as they must have room to expand when they cook.
5. Hang the sausages in a home-style smoker and smoke them for about 1 hour. Ensure the temperature of the smoker does not exceed 150°F.
6. Remove the sausages and hang over a stick or dowel. Place the stick in a cool place and position an electric fan so that it blows directly on the sausages. Allow them to dry for 2 days. They are ready for use.
7. Store them in the refrigerator, where they will keep well for about a week.
